name: DAPG biocontrol sensor (PhlF + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects 2,4-diacetylphloroglucinol (DAPG), an antibiotic made by biocontrol pseudomonads in soil. The Marionette PhlF sensor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in the soil chassis P. putida KT2440 — useful for monitoring soil biocontrol activity.

input.detects: DAPG, an antifungal antibiotic produced by biocontrol Pseudomonas in the rhizosphere

sensing.sensorMechanism: PhlF de-represses its promoter on binding DAPG.
sensing.signalFlow: DAPG -> PhlF releases PphlF -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> CRISPRi represses a constitutive amilCP cassette -> pigment fades with DAPG (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

parts.name: PhlF regulator
parts.source: Marionette sensor (Meyer et al. 2019); Pseudomonas phl system
parts.notes: DAPG-responsive repressor; a widely used orthogonal regulator.

output.result: Pigment change reports soil DAPG (biocontrol activity).

performance.notes: PhlF is a characterised orthogonal Marionette repressor; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 certified HV1 safety host; add containment for field use.
safety.notes: Soil chassis suited to rhizosphere monitoring; instrument-free readout.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PhlF + PphlF -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP. SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Transform P. putida KT2440
buildSteps.description: Electroporate; select; confirm baseline pigment without DAPG.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add soil eluate + DAPG standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the Marionette PhlF DAPG sensor with P. putida CRISPRi
partsSources: Meyer et al. 2019, Nat. Chem. Biol. (Marionette sensors, PhlF)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
